summary: A new international report led by Tzula B. Propp and Brandy Todd from Diversity in Quantum and the Quantum Flagship Equity, Diversity, and Inclusion Working Group highlights ongoing diversity issues in quantum science. Surveying over 1000 quantum scientists globally, findings show that marginalized individuals (women, LGBTQ+, BIPOC, and those from the global south) experience more hardship and are significantly more likely to consider leaving the field (mean score 3.12 versus 2.05 for non-marginalized, p<.001). Perceptions of career opportunity are also lower for marginalized respondents (3.54 versus 3.99, p<0.001). The authors argue these disparities undermine talent retention and the global resilience of the quantum workforce, calling for evidence-driven diversity and inclusion initiatives.
